mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near multi-year lows (currently 1.41%) signals accommodative monetary policy that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window from a low base (currently 4.1%), it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE trigger fires, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS staying flat while CPIAUCSL accelerates causes real policy rates to fall, effectively easing financial conditions even without a nominal rate cut; this combination produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS change, implying declining real rate
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with stable nominal rates
failure_condition: The interaction term (implied real rate change) shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS or CPIAUCSL alone (partial Rank IC < 0.02), or the sign of the relationship reverses across two consecutive 2-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime, a decline in HOUST (currently 1192k units) below its trailing 12-month average signals contracting housing demand that propagates through consumer wealth and construction employment channels, dragging S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next quarter; this effect is muted or absent in easing regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below trailing 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is rising or elevated
failure_condition: During tightening regimes with HOUST below its 12-month average, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.02) when restricting the sample to tightening periods only.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO (currently 107.24) reflects expanding industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, producing a persistent short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as the market incorporates incremental production data.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO readings are followed by negative returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the 3-month annualized growth rate of PAYEMS (currently 147,810k) below 1% signals weakening labor demand that precedes reduced consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the PAYEMS deceleration trigger is active, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising RSAFS (currently 492,003 million) and stagnant or falling PAYEMS indicates consumers are drawing down savings or credit to sustain spending without matching income growth, an unsustainable demand pattern that reverses within 20 trading days, producing negative S&P 500 returns once payroll growth confirms the divergence.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds PAYEMS 3-month growth by more than 2 standard deviations
failure_condition: The RSAFS-PAYEMS divergence signal fails to produce negative returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no predictive power over either series individually (incremental Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated above 3% while FEDFUNDS is below levels consistent with historical Taylor-rule responses, markets anticipate future tightening, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter to be negative as valuation multiples compress in anticipation of rate hikes; this relationship only holds in high-inflation regimes and reverses in low-inflation regimes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 2%
regime_hint: high inflation with lagging policy rate (pre-tightening regime)
failure_condition: During periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% YoY with FEDFUNDS below 2%, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across two consecutive out-of-sample high-inflation episodes.
